reasoning: Kateryna Lagno's Elo rating on 2025-04-17 (and April 2025 overall, as she withdrew from the major FIDE Women's Grand Prix event scheduled for mid-April) is 2516. To achieve a 1% increase, her rating must reach at least 2541.16 (essentially 2542). Gaining over 25 rating points is extremely difficult for established top-10 female players, who have stable ratings and face tough opposition; with a K-factor of 10, gaining 26 points would require outperforming expected scores by 2.6 full points, which usually requires dominating an extended string of elite tournaments. Lagno is 35 years old and has very stable ratings. Moreover, she recently withdrew from a major FIDE event in India in protest, pointing to deep inactivity in the short term and a likely reduction in her tournament play across 2025 entirely. Because FIDE rating lists update monthly, the May list (used for both the May 4 and May 27 dates) will almost certainly show her at an unchanged 2516, putting those probabilities at the absolute minimum (0.02). Moving out to July and October, her possibilities of playing and performing spectacularly increase slightly due to accumulating time and potential domestic or non-boycotted events, but the odds of gaining 26 points remain incredibly slim. Thus, the probabilities sit near the lower bound allowed.
